senses.definition: Changed in mineral composition so that albite has replaced other feldspar minerals in a rock.
examples: The granite was heavily albitised along the main fault zone.
These albitised veins indicate intense fluid activity during metamorphism.

senses.definition: Describing rock or minerals that have undergone replacement by albite.
examples: Albitised plagioclase is common in this intrusion.
The albitised samples show a lighter colour and finer texture.

senses.definition: Third person singular form of a verb meaning to change a rock or mineral so that albite replaces the original feldspar.
examples: The circulating brine albitises the original plagioclase.
This process albitises the host rock along fracture zones.

senses.definition: Present participle form meaning causing albite to replace other feldspar minerals in a rock.
examples: Hydrothermal fluids were albitising the granite during mineralization.
The albitising of feldspars produced a pale, bleached appearance.

senses.definition: Describing fluids or processes that cause albite to replace other minerals.
examples: Albitising solutions moved along the fracture network.
The albitising fluids were rich in sodium.

senses.definition: A light-coloured igneous rock made mostly of the feldspar mineral albite.
examples: The ore is hosted in a fine-grained albitite.
Albitite dykes cut through the older granitoid body.

senses.definition: A geological process where albite replaces plagioclase or other minerals in igneous rocks, often due to hot fluids.
examples: Widespread albitization affected the granite after intrusion.
Albitization is common in altered granitic and volcanic rocks.

senses.definition: To change a rock or mineral so that albite replaces an earlier feldspar or other minerals.
examples: Hydrothermal fluids can albitize granitic plutons over large areas.
Late-stage solutions albitize the feldspathic groundmass.

senses.definition: Past form of a verb meaning to have caused albite to replace other minerals in a rock.
examples: Hydrothermal activity albitized much of the original plagioclase.
Circulating brines albitized the lower part of the intrusion.

senses.definition: Describing rock or minerals that have been changed so they consist mainly of albite.
examples: The albitized rocks are pale and fine-grained.
Albitized plagioclase occurs along all fracture planes.

senses.definition: Third person singular form of a verb meaning to alter rock so that albite replaces earlier minerals.
examples: The hot brine albitizes the surrounding granite.
This process albitizes feldspar near the vein walls.

senses.definition: Present participle form meaning altering rock so that albite replaces its original minerals.
examples: Fluids were albitizing the country rock during mineral deposition.
The albitizing of feldspar occurred in several stages.

senses.definition: A porphyritic igneous rock whose main feldspar mineral is albite.
examples: The ore is associated with an albitophyre dyke swarm.
Albitophyre intrusions cut the volcanic sequence.

senses.definition: A fast-growing tropical or subtropical tree or shrub of the genus Albizia, often grown for shade or as an ornamental plant.
examples: An albizia tree shaded the courtyard café.
Albizia is often planted along roads for quick shade.

senses.definition: Herbal medicine made from parts of Albizia trees, used in some traditional medical systems.
examples: In traditional Chinese medicine, albizia is used to calm the mind.
Some supplements combine albizia with other relaxing herbs.

senses.definition: Plural form of albizia, meaning several trees or shrubs of the genus Albizia.
examples: Albizias line the driveway, creating a cool tunnel of shade.
In this region, albizias are used as windbreaks on farms.

senses.definition: An older spelling of albizia, referring to trees and shrubs of the genus Albizia in warm regions.
examples: The garden features an albizzia with pink, silky flowers.
Albizzia is sometimes called mimosa in cultivation.

senses.definition: Plural form of albizzia, meaning several trees or shrubs of the genus Albizia.
examples: The hillside is dotted with tall albizzias.
Albizias and albizzias are common in this subtropical valley.

senses.definition: An early type of incandescent gas mantle made using calcium carbide and other materials, formerly used for lighting.
examples: Albocarbon was once advertised as a brighter, cleaner light source.
Old catalogs list albocarbon alongside other gas-lighting systems.

senses.definition: White and ash-grey in colour.
examples: The bird displayed albocinereous plumage on its breast.
An albocinereous haze hung over the distant hills.

senses.definition: A white-coloured coccus-shaped bacterium.
examples: The culture revealed an albococcus under the microscope.
Albococcus species were isolated from the sample.

senses.definition: Rule or domination by white people.
examples: The writer condemned the old system as a brutal albocracy.
Critics argued that the colony was effectively an albocracy.

senses.definition: A male given name, especially associated with a 6th‑century king of the Lombards.
examples: Alboin is remembered as a key figure in Lombard history.
The legend of King Alboin was retold in medieval chronicles.

senses.definition: A lightweight building material made from mineral binders mixed with wood shavings or other light fillers.
examples: The cottage walls were made of albolite blocks.
Albolite offers good thermal insulation at low cost.

senses.definition: A kind of white cement or plaster, sometimes used as a trade name for a building material.
examples: The floor was finished with an albolith surface.
Early modernist buildings often used albolith for interior walls.

senses.definition: A white or pale tannin-like substance found in certain plants, used in tanning or dyeing.
examples: The bark contains albopannin, which can be used in leather tanning.
Albopannin gives the extract its mild astringent quality.

senses.definition: Covered with a white, powdery coating.
examples: The lichen has an albopruinose surface under magnification.
Albopruinose patches appeared on the old paintwork.

senses.definition: A song or piece of music performed at dawn, especially as a serenade.
examples: The guitarist played an alborada beneath her window.
The composer wrote an alborada to capture the colours of sunrise.

senses.definition: In Islamic tradition, the miraculous steed that carried the Prophet Muhammad on his night journey.
examples: Stories describe Alborak as a shining, winged creature.
The artist painted the Prophet riding Alborak through the night sky.

senses.definition: A type of volcanic rock rich in feldspar, first described from the Alboran Sea region.
examples: The island is composed mainly of alboranite.
Alboranite samples were collected from the submarine volcano.

senses.definition: A male given name of German origin.
examples: Albrecht grew up in a small Bavarian town.
The painting was signed simply with the name Albrecht.

senses.definition: Used especially to refer to the German Renaissance artist Albrecht Dürer.
examples: Albrecht is famous for his detailed engravings.
In art history, Albrecht is studied alongside Italian masters.

senses.definition: An exclamation of joy or congratulations, borrowed from Spanish.
examples: “Albricias!” she cried when she heard the good news.
He greeted the announcement with a delighted “Albricias!”.

senses.definition: A family name, especially known from figures such as Madeleine Albright, former U.S. Secretary of State.
examples: Albright was the first woman to serve as U.S. Secretary of State.
The lecture quoted Albright on diplomacy and power.

senses.definition: A strong bronze alloy containing aluminium, used for parts that must resist wear and corrosion.
examples: The propeller was cast in albronze for extra durability.
Albronze is often chosen for marine fittings.

senses.definition: A legendary Germanic wise woman or seer, mentioned in some historical and literary sources.
examples: The tribe sought advice from an albruna before battle.
Stories of the albruna blend history and myth.

senses.definition: Long white robes worn by priests and some other ministers in Christian churches, especially during services.
examples: The deacons put on their albs before the procession.
In the sacristy, several freshly washed albs hung on a rail.

senses.definition: A plant of the genus Albuca, with bulb and often spiral or drooping leaves, grown as an ornamental.
examples: She keeps an albuca on the sunny kitchen windowsill.
Albuca bulbs are popular with collectors of unusual succulents.

senses.definition: A former family name for certain parasitic fungi that cause white rust on plants.
examples: Older texts place these organisms in Albuginaceae.
Albuginaceae species attack many crop plants.

senses.definition: A firm white layer of fibrous tissue that forms the outer covering of some organs, such as the testis, ovary, or part of the eye.
examples: The surgeon carefully incised the albuginea of the testis.
Damage to the albuginea can affect the structural support of the organ.

senses.definition: Relating to or resembling the white, fibrous tunica albuginea of an organ.
examples: The albugineous layer surrounds and supports the testicular tissue.
An albugineous capsule encloses the organ.

senses.definition: Plural form of albugo, referring to white patches or spots on a body part such as the eye.
examples: Multiple albugines were visible on the cornea.
The condition left small albugines on the patient’s eye.

senses.definition: Inflammation of the tunica albuginea of an organ.
examples: Albuginitis can cause severe local pain.
The diagnosis of albuginitis was confirmed by imaging.

senses.definition: A white spot or patch on the eye or other body part, often due to disease.
examples: The old injury left an albugo on his cornea.
An albugo was visible when the eyelid was lifted.

senses.definition: A plant disease that produces a white, rust‑like growth on leaves and stems, caused by certain parasitic fungi.
examples: The crop showed signs of albugo on the lower leaves.
Albugo can spread quickly in damp conditions.

senses.definition: Plural form of albugo, meaning several white patches on an eye or other body part.
examples: Multiple albugos formed after repeated infections.
The doctor noted two small albugos on the cornea.

senses.definition: A collection of recorded music released together, usually on a CD or online.
examples: Her latest album went straight to number one.
The band is working on a new studio album.

senses.definition: A book with empty or prepared pages used to keep things such as photos, stamps, or autographs.
examples: He showed me an old photo album from his childhood.
She keeps her stamp collection in a special album.

senses.definition: A short piece of music, often simple and lyrical, written for a friend’s or patron’s album.
examples: The piano piece is a charming little Albumblatt.
Composers of the Romantic era often wrote an Albumblatt as a gift.

senses.definition: Plural form of Albumblatt, meaning short, often intimate pieces of music composed for an album.
examples: The collection includes several charming Albumblatter.
These Albumblatter were dedicated to the composer’s friends.

senses.definition: An alternative English plural of Albumblatt, referring to several short musical pieces written for albums.
examples: The pianist ended the recital with three gentle albumblatts.
Publishers sometimes grouped albumblatts into sets.

senses.definition: Made of or like albumen, especially in being clear and egg‑white‑like.
examples: The liquid formed an albumean layer on the surface.
An albumean film coated the inside of the shell.

senses.definition: The clear part of an egg that surrounds the yolk; egg white.
examples: Beat the albumen until it forms stiff peaks.
The recipe calls for two yolks and one albumen.

senses.definition: A general term for water‑soluble proteins found in blood, eggs, and other tissues.
examples: Serum albumen levels were measured before surgery.
Albumen plays a role in maintaining blood volume.

senses.definition: A device or substance used to convert something into an albumen form or to treat it with albumen.
examples: The laboratory used an albumeniizer to prepare the photographic plates.
An albumeniizer helps coat the surface evenly.

senses.definition: The process of treating a surface with albumen, especially in early photographic techniques.
examples: Albumenisation of the paper gave the print a glossy finish.
The manual describes the correct method of albumenisation.

senses.definition: To coat or impregnate something with albumen.
examples: Photographers used to albumenise the paper before printing.
You must albumenise the surface to achieve the right sheen.

senses.definition: Past form of a verb meaning to have coated or treated something with albumen.
examples: They albumenised the paper before exposing it to light.
The plates were carefully albumenised in the darkroom.

senses.definition: Describing paper, plates, or another surface that has been coated with albumen.
examples: The albumenised paper produced a rich image.
Museum collections still hold albumenised photographs.
